title: ranunculus flammula subsp. minimus
intro: Ranunculus flammula subsp. minimus, also known as lesser water buttercup, is a subspecies of the water buttercup plant, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and ability to thrive in moist environments. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been used to treat various ailments such as skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus flammula subsp. minimus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote healing and well-being.
